blocks.text: DTC P0011: VTC system function abnormal

The continuously variable valve timing control mechanism (VTC) hydraulically operates the VTC actuator to control the intake camshaft phase in order to obtain optimum valve timing The PGM-FI ECU determines that the difference between the intake camshaft phase control command value and the actual intake cam If the difference between the shaft phase value and the actual intake camshaft phase value continues for a set period of time at or above the set value, the PGM-FI ECU determines that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.

blocks.text: Intake If the difference between the camshaft phase command value and the actual intake camshaft phase value remains at 20° or more for 15 seconds or longer.

items.text: Response delay of VTC hydraulic valve
Short downstream of VTC hydraulic valve
Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 40 km/h-100 km/h for at least 15 seconds at a constant speed.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: Run the VTC test in the Inspection menu with HDS.

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
